name: Tocantins (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the central diagonal band, evoking sunlight and peace
the cerrado's earth and the rising sun

description: A diagonal field split into a blue upper-hoist triangle and an orange lower-fly triangle, separated by a wide white wedge that radiates from the lower hoist toward the upper fly.
history: Adopted on 1 January 1989 alongside the creation of the State of Tocantins by the 1988 Brazilian Constitution; the flag was designed by sociologist Paulo Brito and approved by the first state constituent assembly.

features.symbolism: A diagonal split with blue in the upper hoist quadrant and orange in the lower fly, divided by a wedge-shaped white band; the colours represent the sky, the dry earth of the cerrado, and the sun-bleached light of central Brazil
